ideas.headline: OPEKA (now OPA) Benefit-Claim Copilot survives hostile second-pass: UK Caddy reached 40+ Citizens Advice offices with a 2025 RCT showing 80% approval and drafting time halved, giving the OPA Governor a defensible 'why-now' brief; single-buyer απευθείας ανάθεση path, deep KYA-citation moat, and a recruitable Greek admin-law + caseworker founder pair make this the ministry's lead bet.
blockers: OPEKA was renamed to OPA in the 2026 reorganisation — pitch deck and contract templates must reflect the new entity; verify which DPO and legal directorate now owns benefit-decision data-sharing agreements.

EU AI Act Annex III classifies social-benefits eligibility as high-risk; obligations apply Aug 2026 unless the Digital Omnibus delays to Dec 2027. Either way, a DPIA + AI-Act traceability log must be designed in from day 1 and the human-in-the-loop architecture must be auditable.

Cross-system data pulls (EFKA contributions, AADE income, Interior household composition) need formal inter-agency agreements under L.4624/2019; clearance loop is the real critical path, not the model.

Tier-1 SI (Netcompany/Intrasoft footprint on OPEKA case-management) will lobby to absorb the copilot as a change request — needs Governor-level air cover for a productivity-layer carve-out before kickoff.
ideas.next30days: Book a 45-min meeting with the OPA Governor through a Social Cohesion advisor warm intro to pre-register a 10-caseworker pilot on a single benefit family (επίδομα παιδιού A21 is the obvious wedge given the Feb-Mar 2026 application window just closed). Lock 3 KPIs (median days-to-decision, draft-to-issue median, appeal-overturn rate held flat). In parallel, recruit one named ex-OPEKA/EFKA caseworke

ideas.headline: GenY Birth-Incentive Eligibility Agent upgrades to conditional: France CAF prime à la naissance is now fully auto-evaluated on pregnancy declaration (2026 amount €1,093.11), Estonia Bürokratt's birth life-event service is operational with a 2026 pivot to per-institution personalised AI agents, and Ireland's auto-enrolment holds — fresh precedents stack neatly. Binding constraint is the 75k-births/
blockers: Standalone TAM is structurally narrow (~75k birth events/year × small per-event take-up gap) — pilot procurability is fine but the venture-scale story works only if the agent is positioned as the wedge into the broader family-navigator (Idea 3) within 18 months.

Wallet.gov.gr consent flow at the maternity ward is the real critical path; if Digital Governance ministry won't expose the birth-event trigger as an API, the agent is reduced to a post-hoc reminder system and loses the 'proactive' narrative.

GS Demographics champion is friendly but the PM-office demographic-policy KPI is a politically owned headline number — any agent that surfaces uptake gaps before being asked could embarrass the sponsor; framing must lead with 'closing the gap your policy already pays for'.
France CAF's substrate is centralised; Greece's EFKA + AADE + OPA + Interior split means the orchestration layer is harder than the French precedent suggests at first glance.
ideas.next30days: Pull (or commission) the published uptake-vs-entitlement gap on the επίδομα γέννησης since 2020 — that single chart is the entire pitch. In parallel, get a 30-minute exploratory with the GS Demographics & Family advisor team to test framing as a demographic-policy win, and a separate 30-min with a Wallet.gov.gr product owner to scope the birth-event trigger API.

ideas.headline: Family Benefit Eligibility Navigator holds at conditional: France mes-aides remains the global reference, Ireland's MyWelfare 'Benefit-of-Work Estimator' is live in 2026, and Estonia's 2026 personalised-AI-agent pivot validates the architecture — but two-ministry sponsorship (Social Cohesion + Digital Governance) and the €300-700k full-ΕΣΗΔΗΣ/RRF pilot envelope keep this firmly behind the OPA copi
blockers: Channel-conflict with gov.gr is the textbook two-ministry friction: Digital Governance has a legitimate ownership claim on any citizen-facing entitlement layer, and unless a paired sponsorship memo is in hand on day one, the project becomes a Ψηφιακή Διακυβέρνηση absorption candidate within 12 months.
Pilot tier (€300-700k) is structurally above απευθείας ανάθεση and either needs an RRF demographics-envelope carve-out or a full ΕΣΗΔΗΣ — both add 6-12 months to time-to-pilot.

Citizen-data consent flow under GDPR + L.4624/2019 + Wallet.gov.gr must be designed before any cross-agency data pull; the Ireland MyWelfare MyGovID pattern is a clean template but the Greek Wallet equivalent is still maturing.
Founder pair (Greek consumer-product builder + family-policy lawyer) is recruitable in Athens but every credible candidate is currently being pursued by other studio bets; window is real.
ideas.next30days: Do not solo-pitch. Use the 30 days to draft the two-ministry paired sponsorship memo (Social Cohesion owns eligibility-logic + demographic-uptake KPI; Digital Governance owns gov.gr channel) and circulate it to a Social Cohesion advisor for a temperature check before any external work. Gate further investment to that memo being formally received.

ideas.headline: Disability-Services Matcher downgrades to fail under the hostile pass: scope is exactly the disability/vulnerable-populations third-rail the studio brief says to avoid; Spain's Dependencia and the UK Carer's Allowance pilots are real but show no 2025-2026 AI-matching deployment Greece can cite; E.S.A.M.E.A. relationships and Head-of-Disability-Sector founder bench are not in T-Life's network.
blockers: Studio-fit (axis 6) is the binding kill: brief explicitly excludes disability/vulnerable-populations as a fundamental-rights third-rail; even a 'light-touch' matcher inherits E.S.A.M.E.A. scrutiny and EU AI Act Annex III essential-public-services classification.

Buyer is structurally fragmented: Social Cohesion disability directorate sets policy, KEPA (Labour ministry) certifies, municipalities deliver — three-way coordination plus E.S.A.M.E.A. as an effective veto-holder makes a 6-month pilot impossible.

Founder-market-fit (axis 3): no candidate in T-Life's network with both NGO/E.S.A.M.E.A. credibility AND the engineering/product chops; building that bench is 12+ months and would crowd out OPA copilot recruitment.

Precedent freshness is thin: Spain's July 2025 dependency-law reform is procedural not AI; SETWorks 2026 'AI in Disability Services' is vendor content not a government deployment; no operational national disability-matching agent has come online in 2025-2026 that Greece can point to.

EU AI Act Annex III treats AI used to determine access to essential public services for vulnerable populations as high-risk — full conformity assessment, ethics board, and disability-advocate observer are non-negotiable, which pushes pilot budget to €500k-1M+.
ideas.next30days: Do not pursue. If a municipal champion (KEDE-backed mayor) personally commissions, revisit only as a paid feasibility study (<€20k) bounded to one δήμος and one service type — and only after the OPA copilot has signed.

ideas.headline: Foster-Care Matching confirmed fail on hostile pass: US Casebook/Binti/CareMatch are commercially live in 2026 (so 'blue ocean' is qualified), but EU AI Act Annex III high-risk obligations for children-affecting AI plus judicial-process integration plus a non-existent founder bench keep this firmly outside T-Life's pilot-tier capital and time-to-market thesis.
blockers: Studio-fit (axis 6): children-affecting algorithmic decisions are exactly the brief's named third-rail; one mistaken placement that hits the press ends both the company and the ministry official's career — the asymmetric downside is total.

EU AI Act Annex III applies (vulnerable populations, essential services) with high-risk obligations from Aug 2026 (Dec 2027 if Digital Omnibus delay passes); full conformity-assessment package + child-protection ethics board + judicial sign-off on methodology before pilot pushes cost to €800k-2M and timeline to 18+ months.
Buyer convening power required (GS Child Protection + Family Court President + UNICEF/Save the Children observer) is beyond T-Life seed-stage capacity.
Founder bench is empty: no Head of Child Protection / senior social-worker / family-law judge with engineering co-founder available in network.

Casebook, Binti and CareMatch are live US commercial products; Greek-language moat exists but is not enough to offset the compliance and capital constraints — the 'no Greek incumbent' framing is real but irrelevant to studio fit.
ideas.next30days: Do not pursue. Re-open only if (a) a named GS Child Protection commissions in writing with explicit AI-Act conformity budget line, (b) a Family-Court-bench advisor is signed as paid founding advisor, AND (c) a UNICEF or Save-the-Children observer is named — none of which are achievable within the studio's current convening surface.
